steps.title: Identify what “This relative scarcity” refers to
steps.content:
The underlined phrase is a pronoun reference: “This relative scarcity” points back to something stated earlier. The previous sentence says plant-based artifacts “are seldom studied due to their perishable nature” — that is the scarcity. It is a difficult situation for researchers, not a failed goal, an uncertain discovery, or a contrast between artifact types.
  • Choice A — ‘It suggests that the researchers did not fully achieve a goal outlined earlier in the text.’: No goal is outlined and then failed; the text celebrates a surprising discovery. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘It describes the uncertain nature of a discovery by the researchers mentioned earlier in the text.’: The scarcity refers to available artifacts in general, not to uncertainty about one specific discovery. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘It distinguishes between the two types of historical artifacts discussed earlier in the text.’: The phrase does not contrast stone tools and plant-based items; it describes how rare plant-based artifacts are. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘It refers to a challenging situation presented earlier in the text.’: The scarcity of plant-based artifacts is exactly the challenging situation described in the prior sentence. ✓

steps.title: Confirm the correct choice
steps.content: The underlined phrase points back to the perishability problem — a challenging situation — and that scarcity is what “made it all the more exciting” when the team found well-preserved specimens. That confirms D is correct.
